record_office_terminology.land_records: County Auditor (deeds, mortgages, plats). Washington uses the Auditor rather than a Recorder for land records.
record_office_terminology.probate: County Superior Court (probate, guardianship, adoption). Some counties have separate Clerk of Superior Court offices.
record_office_terminology.marriage_licenses: County Auditor issues marriage licenses. Marriage certificates filed with the County Auditor.
record_office_terminology.vital_records_state_start: 1907 (state registration). County auditors recorded vital events before 1907, with varying compliance. Seattle and some cities kept their own records earlier.

burned_county_authority: Washington State Archives Research Guides; FamilySearch Wiki — Washington. Washington has no burned counties, but some territorial-era records are sparse due to low population and informal record-keeping before statehood.

burned_counties_summary.notes: Washington has no burned counties. However, the great Seattle fire of 1889 destroyed some King County business records. Some territorial-era county records are incomplete due to sparse settlement and informal government before 1889 statehood.

administrative_notes: Washington Territory was created in 1853 from the northern portion of Oregon Territory. It became the 42nd state on November 11, 1889. The territory originally included present-day Idaho and parts of Montana until 1863. Washington has 39 counties. County Auditors maintain land records and issue marriage licenses. The Washington State Archives in Olympia is the central repository, with regional branches in Ellensburg (Central), Bellingham (Northwest), and Cheney (Eastern). The Washington State Historical Society in Tacoma holds significant manuscript and photograph collections. Donation Land Claims (1850-1855) are important for early settlers. Hudson's Bay Company records document the earliest Euro-American presence at Fort Vancouver and Fort Nisqually.

core_repositories.description: Central state repository holding territorial records from 1853, state government records, county records on microfilm, naturalization records, prison records, and state institutional records. The Digital Archives provides online access to many collections including birth, marriage, and death indexes.

Free online portal providing searchable indexes and images for Washington vital records, county records, military records, naturalization records, and institutional records. Contains millions of indexed records including marriage, birth, and death records.

core_repositories.source_name: Washington State Archives — Central Regional Branch (Ellensburg)
core_repositories.description: Regional branch holding county records for Benton, Chelan, Douglas, Franklin, Grant, Kittitas, Klickitat, Okanogan, Walla Walla, and Yakima counties. Includes court records, naturalization papers, and county government documents.

core_repositories.source_name: Washington State Archives — Northwest Regional Branch (Bellingham)
core_repositories.description: Regional branch holding county records for Island, San Juan, Skagit, Snohomish, and Whatcom counties. Strong for Puget Sound maritime records and immigration documentation.

core_repositories.source_name: Washington State Archives — Eastern Regional Branch (Cheney)
core_repositories.description: Regional branch holding county records for Adams, Asotin, Columbia, Ferry, Garfield, Lincoln, Pend Oreille, Spokane, Stevens, Wahkiakum, and Whitman counties. Strong for eastern Washington agricultural and mining records.

Major research library holding manuscript collections, photograph archives, maps, oral histories, and personal papers related to Washington history. Strong for Pacific Northwest fur trade, timber industry, and labor history collections.

familysearch_collections.description: Microfilmed and digitized Washington county records including marriage records, vital records, probate files, and land records from territorial era through mid-20th century. Extensive microfilm holdings from Washington State Archives.
Indexed state death records from the beginning of state registration. Includes name, date, place of death, and other details useful for linking family members.

newspapers_and_periodicals.description: Digitized historic Washington newspapers including the Seattle Post-Intelligencer, Tacoma Daily Ledger, Spokane Falls Review, Olympia Washington Standard, Walla Walla Statesman, and numerous small-town weeklies from the territorial period through the early 20th century.
Microfilm collection of Washington newspapers not yet digitized, including rural weeklies, ethnic press (Chinese, Japanese, Scandinavian), and labor newspapers from the timber and fishing industries.

military_records.description: State militia and National Guard records, territorial volunteer records, WWI and WWII service cards, and veterans' discharge records filed with county auditors. Washington contributed troops from the Indian Wars through modern conflicts.

military_records.source_name: Fort Lewis / Joint Base Lewis-McChord Records
military_records.description: Military personnel and installation records from Fort Lewis (established 1917), one of the largest military bases on the West Coast. Records of soldiers and their families stationed at the base, including WWII mobilization records.

land_and_property.description: Donation Land Claims filed under the 1850 Donation Land Claim Act in Washington Territory. These pre-date the Homestead Act and document married couples (wives named in claims), providing crucial genealogical links for early settlers north of the Columbia River.

land_and_property.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Washington
land_and_property.description: Federal land patents, homestead entries, cash sales, and military bounty warrants for Washington Territory and State. Searchable online database with images of original patent documents.

vital_records.source_name: Washington State Department of Health — Vital Records
vital_records.description: State vital records office holding birth and death certificates from 1907. Marriage records available through county auditors. Certified copies available for eligible applicants. Historical records gradually being digitized through Washington Digital Archives.

vital_records.source_name: Washington Digital Archives — Vital Records Indexes
vital_records.description: Free searchable indexes for Washington birth, marriage, and death records. Coverage varies by county and time period. Marriage records are particularly strong with many counties having records from the 1850s-1860s.

church_and_cemetery.repository: Find A Grave / BillionGraves / WA GenWeb
church_and_cemetery.description: Online cemetery transcriptions and photograph projects covering Washington cemeteries. Many pioneer cemeteries in rural areas have been documented by local genealogical societies. Includes logging camp burials and company town cemeteries.

Records from Methodist, Catholic, Presbyterian, and Lutheran churches that were among the earliest established in Washington Territory. Catholic mission records (especially Oblates of Mary Immaculate) document both settler and Native American populations. Scandinavian Lutheran congregations particularly strong in Puget Sound area.

research_guides_and_societies.description: State-level genealogical society coordinating with county chapters. Publishes the Washington State Genealogical Society Newsletter and maintains research guides. Links to county-level societies throughout the state.
Major genealogical society with extensive research library. Holdings include Washington county histories, family files, and periodicals. Publishes the Seattle Genealogical Society Bulletin.

historical_counties_extinct.notes: Original territorial county; records continued under Mason County.

historical_counties_extinct.disposition: Renamed to Kitsap County region; absorbed into other jurisdictions
historical_counties_extinct.notes: Early territorial county; boundaries changed frequently in the 1850s–1860s.

historical_counties_extinct.disposition: Renamed to Grays Harbor County in 1915
historical_counties_extinct.notes: Researchers must check records under both Chehalis County (pre-1915) and Grays Harbor County (post-1915).

historical_counties_extinct.disposition: Absorbed into Walla Walla and other eastern counties
historical_counties_extinct.notes: Short-lived territorial county covering part of eastern Washington.

native_american_and_removal_records.source_name: Bureau of Indian Affairs — Pacific Northwest Region Records
native_american_and_removal_records.description: BIA records for Washington tribes including the Yakama, Tulalip, Colville, Quinault, Makah, Puyallup, Muckleshoot, Nisqually, Swinomish, Lummi, Nooksack, Spokane, and many others. Includes allotment records, census rolls, annuity payment records, and agency correspondence.

Annual census rolls for Washington reservations including Colville, Yakama, Tulalip, Quinault, Makah, Puyallup, Chehalis, Skokomish, and others. Cover approximately 1885–1940 and list tribal members by name, age, and family relationship.

native_american_and_removal_records.source_name: Treaty Records — Washington Territory Indian Treaties (1854–1856)
native_american_and_removal_records.description: Records related to the Stevens Treaties (1854–1856) negotiated by Governor Isaac Stevens with numerous Washington tribes. Include treaty texts, council proceedings, and related correspondence documenting tribal signatories and their communities.

immigration_and_naturalization.description: Naturalization records from Washington territorial and state courts including declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Many are searchable through the Digital Archives. Cover Scandinavian, Chinese, Japanese, Filipino, and European immigrants.

immigration_and_naturalization.source_name: Chinese Exclusion Act Case Files — Seattle/Tacoma
immigration_and_naturalization.description: Case files for Chinese residents in Washington under the Chinese Exclusion Act (1882–1943). Include photographs, witness testimony, and family information. Particularly significant for Seattle and Tacoma, which experienced anti-Chinese riots in 1885–1886.

Records related to the forced removal and internment of Japanese Americans from Washington during WWII. Includes WRA records, camp rosters, property inventories, and the Densho digital archive of oral histories and primary sources. Covers communities from White River Valley, Bainbridge Island, Seattle, and the Yakima Valley.

immigration_and_naturalization.repository: Nordic Heritage Museum / University of Washington
immigration_and_naturalization.description: Records documenting Norwegian, Swedish, Danish, Finnish, and Icelandic immigration to the Puget Sound region. Includes church records, fraternal organization records, and community newspapers in Scandinavian languages.
